### Post by schnauzer93 on 2008-03-26
When I try to play games such as Urban Terror and Wolfenstein: Enemy Territory, the graphics look very... sketchy, with dark areas, flashing players, etc. 

My xorg.conf looks like this:```
# xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier      "Generic Keyboard"
        Driver          "kbd"
        Option          "CoreKeyboard"
        Option          "XkbRules"      "xorg"
        Option          "XkbModel"      "pc105"
        Option          "XkbLayout"     "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier      "Configured Mouse"
        Driver          "mouse"
        Option          "CorePointer"
        Option          "Device"        "/dev/input/mice"
        Option          "Protocol"      "ImPS/2"
        Option          "ZAxisMapping"  "4 5"
        Option          "Emulate3Buttons"       "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Driver          "wacom"
        Identifier      "stylus"
        Option          "Device"        "/dev/input/wacom"
        Option          "Type"  "stylus"
        Option          "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Driver          "wacom"
        Identifier      "eraser"
        Option          "Device"        "/dev/input/wacom"
        Option          "Type"  "eraser"
        Option          "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Driver          "wacom"
        Identifier      "cursor"
        Option          "Device"        "/dev/input/wacom"
        Option          "Type"  "cursor"
        Option          "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
        Identifier      "Generic Video Card"
        Driver          "fglrx"
        Busid           "PCI:1:5:0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier      "Hanns.G HW17"
        Option          "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier      "Default Screen"
        Device          "Generic Video Card"
        Monitor         "Hanns.G HW17"
        Defaultdepth    24
        SubSection "Display"
                Modes           "1440x1440"     "1440x900"      "1400x1050"    "1280x1024"      "1280x960"      "1280x800"      "1152x864"      "1024x768"     "832x624"        "800x600"       "720x400"       "640x480"
        EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
        Identifier      "Default Layout"
  screen "Default Screen"
        Inputdevice     "Generic Keyboard"
        Inputdevice     "Configured Mouse"

        # Uncomment if you have a wacom tablet
        #       InputDevice     "stylus"        "SendCoreEvents"
        #       InputDevice     "cursor"        "SendCoreEvents"
        #       InputDevice     "eraser"        "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection
Section "Module"
        Load            "glx"
EndSection
Section "Extensions"
        Option          "Composite"     "1"
EndSection

```
Also, when I type "glxinfo" into a terminal, I get ```

Xlib:  extension "XFree86-DRI" missing on display ":1.0".

   visual  x  bf lv rg d st colorbuffer ax dp st accumbuffer  ms  cav
 id dep cl sp sz l  ci b ro  r  g  b  a bf th cl  r  g  b  a ns b eat

```
Direct rendering is disabled, which is what I think is causing my difficulties.

fglrxinfo output:```
display: :0.0  screen: 0
OpenGL vendor string: ATI Technologies Inc.
OpenGL renderer string: ATI Radeon X1200 Series
OpenGL version string: 2.0.6473 (8.37.6)

```

I have the restricted drivers installed and enabled. Any ideas?

---

### Post by glennric on 2008-03-26
Try adding 
```
Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection
```
to the end of your xorg.conf file.  Also add  the line
```
load "dri"
```
under the line
```
load "glx"
```
Then restart the xserver and see if that helps.

---

### Post by schnauzer93 on 2008-04-11
It didn't help - Graphics while playing games are still weird, but strangely enough, Compiz hasn't been giving me any problems.

---

### Post by schnauzer93 on 2008-04-12
Ah, I think I've found the problem. When I boot into failsafe GNOME, Direct Rendering switches to "Yes". Also, when I un-install the xserver-xgl package, direct rendering works well when I boot normally However, this disables Compiz. Is there any way I can have both Compiz and the ability to play games?

---

### Post by Crafty Kisses on 2008-04-12
I'm not sure, I usually play games like Open-Arena, and Stepmania just fine with desktop effects on, but when I go to Unreal Tournament 2004 with the desktop effects it starts messing with the game.

Personally, I'd turn them off when I play games. I don't see why you would need them while you're playing the game.

---

### Post by schnauzer93 on 2008-04-12
The graphics stay buggy, even if I turn them off while playing games. It seems that xserver-xgl remains loaded, even with Compiz turned off. If there is no xserver-xgl upgrade, or an alternative, is there any way to create a separate session that has xserver-xgl disabled? In failsafe mode, sound is disabled, so in order to play games with sound, I have to remove the xserver-xgl package entirely.

---
